product_summary.one_sentence_summary: White-label licensing concept for packaged Luxembourg language curricula that schools or NGOs could adapt and resell.
product_summary.target_user: Language schools, integration NGOs and corporate training teams in Luxembourg and nearby border markets.
product_summary.core_pain: Potential licensees want ready-made local-language content instead of building a course from scratch.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: A licensable curriculum package with teacher materials and a branded completion badge.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The offer is a licensing product, so the likely first buyer is a partner organization that would reuse the curriculum.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Corporate training teams can buy a ready-made curriculum instead of building one internally.
Employers or integration sponsors may buy the package for workforce language development.

budget_owner.reason: Budget could sit with a training director, program lead or business owner depending on the licensee.

buyer_influencers.reason: For smaller schools or NGOs, the final buying decision may sit with leadership.
Corporate learning teams may influence whether a ready-made curriculum is useful.
A packaged curriculum often competes with in-house build-or-buy discussions.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Existing curricula can be packaged for licensing to third-party schools and NGOs.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Public sources show Luxembourgish courses and certification routes exist, but they do not validate this exact licensing package.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Educateme Certified badge is an official or externally recognised credential.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: No public source reviewed shows external recognition for the proposed badge.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: No one else can create Luxembourg language curricula at this quality.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: INLL runs Luxembourgish courses and issues the national LAF certification, and the Luxembourg Lifelong Learning Center also offers course and certification programs. The market is not empty.
This is an internal revenue projection, not a publicly verified market fact.
No public source reviewed establishes the economics of this exact licensing package.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Belgium and border-region demand is sufficient to sustain a licensing offer.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Public sources confirm demand for Luxembourgish language learning and continuing training, but not the market size for this exact licensing motion.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Educateme Certified badge will carry market weight.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No official source shows external recognition for the badge.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The curriculum packages are ready for third-party licensing as-is.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The source card is a hypothesis; public evidence does not validate packaging maturity.
This is an internal projection, not an externally verified market result.

regulator_entities.name: Institut national des langues Luxembourg (INLL)
regulator_entities.relevance: National certification body for Luxembourgish language diplomas and courses.

regulator_entities.name: Ministry of the Economy
regulator_entities.relevance: Luxembourg guidance says a business permit can be required for training to third parties outside the company.

framework_entities.name: LAF – Lëtzebuergesch als Friemsprooch
framework_entities.relevance: Official national Luxembourgish certification route; independent of the courses taken.

framework_entities.name: Business permit for continuing professional training establishment
framework_entities.relevance: Luxembourg guidance for organisations providing training to third parties outside the company.

approval_entities.name: Business permit for continuing professional training establishment route
approval_entities.relevance: Relevant route if the provider delivers training to third parties outside the company.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: INLL is the national certification body for Luxembourgish and LAF is independent of courses.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Luxembourgish language certification and prep courses

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Provision of training to third parties outside the company can require a business permit, subject to exemptions.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: INLL Luxembourgish courses and LAF certification
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Official language training and certification route already exists.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Luxembourg Lifelong Learning Center (LLLC)
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public continuing vocational training provider with a broad course and certification offer.

legal_claim_severity.reason: This is mostly a commercial licensing proposition; the strongest claims are soft, not compliance-grade.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Delivery of the licensing concept appears possible with internal expertise, though the actual third-party training route may need a permit.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about official recognition or badge value should be externally reviewed before use.

proof_stack.reason: The first proof is whether schools or NGOs actually want to license the content.
A paid pilot or first license is the clearest commercial validation.
If the content is reused, the licensee should still show learner progress.

proof_burden.reason: The product needs commercial acceptance and basic usage proof, but not regulated acceptance.

dependency_types.reason: Licensees still need capable teachers to deliver the curriculum.
Luxembourgish, French and German course materials need local adaptation.
Domain review helps avoid overclaiming on language and certification value.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The core offer is country-specific and the border-region use case depends on local language and training expectations.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Moving beyond Luxembourg and nearby markets would require substantial localisation of language content and training norms.

subsidy_exists.reason: No funding route was identified as clearly relevant to this licensing concept.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The product card does not rely on a subsidy or reimbursement route.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No meaningful subsidy claim is made for this product.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The offer is an education/licensing product, but the route to third-party training in Luxembourg is not entirely frictionless.

claim_language_risk.reason: Words like certified and unique content can easily overstate externally verifiable value.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: Luxembourg guidance suggests a training-provider permit may be required for third-party training delivery.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Official Luxembourgish courses and certification routes already exist, so the uniqueness claim is not defensible.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The revenue anchor is internal and unverified.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: No subsidy route is being relied upon.

positioning_risk.reason: The product sits between content licensing and training delivery, so the route and ownership boundaries need to be explicit.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: No one else can create Luxembourg language curricula at this quality.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: INLL already offers Luxembourgish courses and the official LAF certification route, and the LLLC also offers broad continuing education. The market is not empty.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Curriculum packages are ready for third-party licensing as-is.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: Luxembourg guidance says training to third parties outside the company can require a business permit, so the route is not frictionless.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Clarify whether the offer is pure IP licensing, training delivery, or a hybrid of both.
Decide whether the Educateme badge is purely internal branding or should be removed from customer-facing copy.

sources.title: Learn to speak Luxembourgish with our language courses
Manager of a continuing professional training establishment
